items.title: Git hooks can be silently bypassed by path-resolution mistakes
items.description: Linked worktrees, custom hooks paths, and quoted filenames can cause a hook to be installed or parsed incorrectly.
items.impact: Sensitive-path and commit-message guardrails may not run.
items.mitigation: Use Git-resolved shared hook paths, reject core.hooksPath, parse staged names as NUL-delimited data, and retain regression coverage.

items.title: External hook managers require manual integration
items.description: Backup hooks and Husky-managed hooks are not automatically chained.
items.impact: A user may assume both hook sets execute when only one owner is active.
items.mitigation: Fail fast on core.hooksPath and document ownership, backup, and manual chaining behavior.
